name: prompts
context: Define MCP prompts — reusable interaction templates that users select to structure conversations with language models
info: This sub-skill covers MCP prompts — the user-controlled primitive. Prompts are reusable templates exposed by MCP servers that structure interactions with language models. They typically surface as slash commands or menu items in host applications. Each prompt has a name, optional description, and optional arguments for customization. When retrieved via prompts/get, they return an array of messages (user/assistant roles) with content blocks (text, images, embedded resources). Python: use @mcp.prompt() decorator. TypeScript: use server.prompt() with Zod schemas. Prompts complement tools and resources by providing pre-built interaction patterns.

sections.title: Defining prompts
sections.body: Python: `@mcp.prompt()` decorator on a function returning string or list of messages. TypeScript: `server.prompt(name, description, zodSchema, handler)` returning `{messages: [...]}`. Arguments are defined by function parameters (Python) or Zod schema (TypeScript). Prompts can be parameterless or accept multiple arguments.

sections.title: Protocol messages
sections.body: `prompts/list` returns available prompts with name, description, and arguments array. `prompts/get` takes name and argument values, returns description and messages array. Arguments support auto-completion via the completion API. `notifications/prompts/list_changed` signals prompt list updates. Supports pagination via cursor.

sections.title: Message content types
sections.body: Prompt messages can contain: text (plain text), image (base64 + mimeType), audio (base64 + mimeType), embedded resource (URI + content). Messages have a `role` field: `user` or `assistant`. Multi-turn prompts return multiple messages to set up conversation context. All content types support optional annotations.

sections.title: Prompt patterns
sections.body: Common patterns: code review prompts, error explanation, document summarization, data analysis, debugging sessions. Multi-turn prompts set up conversation history. Resource-embedded prompts include live data. Dynamic prompts fetch fresh data at retrieval time. Prompts surface as slash commands in most host UIs.

links.context: Tools perform actions; prompts structure interactions
Resources can be embedded in prompt messages

related.note: Resources can be embedded in prompts

scripts.name: Add Prompt to Python Server
scripts.description: Add a prompt definition to an existing FastMCP server

depends.reason: Prompts are defined within an MCP server
